lecture_application: SPY/QQQ and baskets are converted to daily percent changes before covariance/eigendecomposition.
additive_note: Data inventory and PCA metrics preserve the return-based calculation.

lecture_application: Eigenvectors are sign-indeterminate; the index eigen-portfolio is sign-aligned to SPY.
additive_note: Index metrics record the correlation and weight sum.

lecture_application: QQQ-on-SPY OLS slope differs from inverse SPY-on-QQQ slope.
additive_note: PCA metrics record both OLS slopes and the PCA slope.

lecture_application: Crude/gasoline pair is cointegrated, while eigen-portfolio/SPY is highly correlated but not cointegrated.
additive_note: Cointegration table separates p-values from correlation.

lecture_application: Notebook spread tests omit costs, slippage, borrow/funding, stop loss, and sizing constraints.
additive_note: Backtest metrics mark these as demonstration-only.

lecture_application: Top-40 stock basket is a shipped current/selected universe.
additive_note: Production research should use point-in-time constituents.

lecture_application: The notebook uses diff(axis=1)['RB=F']; this depends on CL/RB column order.
additive_note: A production version should name the spread explicitly as RB_z minus CL_z.
